title: About me!
text:
Currently



I’m a fourth-year Environmental Studies B.S. student at UC Santa Barbara, where my research has spanned endangered species conservation, marine debris, geospatial data science, science communication, and environmental education. Outside the classroom, I lead the Spotting Giant Sea Bass Project at the Benioff Ocean Science Laboratory and direct UCSB Sprout Up, a nonprofit bringing environmental education to elementary schools. Head to my Projects page to see more of what I’ve been up to!





All smiles for Campus Point Beach








Upcoming



This coming fall, I’ll be joining the University of Miami’s Rosenstiel School to pursue my PhD in Environmental Science and Policy in Dr. Juan Carlos Villaseñor-Derbez’s Human-Ocean Systems Lab. My research will use data science, spatial analysis, and causal inference to understand how marine policy and environmental change shape human behavior at sea. I’m excited to grow as a researcher and explore the city of Miami!





Go Canes!








Interests



When I’m not in the field or behind a screen, you can find me backpacking, scuba diving, practicing yoga, or listening to live music. I inherited a love for running from my mom, and I completed my first half-marathon in 2024. I’m also an avid reader - check out my GoodReads to see what I’m reading right now!





Reading after completing the Trans-Catalina Trail

title: Macey Hartmann
text:
Hi there! My name is Macey Hartmann. I grew up along the Northern California coast, where the ocean shaped the way I see the world and drives my work in marine conservation and environmental science. This site is a window into my research, projects, and the work I care about most. Thanks for visiting, I hope you find something that interests you!


Education
University of California, Santa Barbara
Bachelor of Science in Environmental Studies
Sept 2022 - June 2026
